card.intuition: A behavioral pattern where an object (the subject) maintains a list of its dependents (observers) and notifies them automatically of any state changes.
card.analogy: A YouTube subscription: the creator (Subject) doesn't personally call every fan to say a video is out. They just 'notify' the platform, and every 'Subscriber' (Observer) gets an alert automatically.
when_to_use: Implementing 'Events' or 'Listeneers' in UI frameworks (clicking a button notifies many components)
Syncing multiple UI views with a single data model
Building a stock market alert system where users watch specific prices
gotchas: Memory Leaks: if you 'Subscribe' but forget to 'Unsubscribe', the Subject will keep a reference to your object forever, preventing it from being garbage collected.
Notification Order: usually, you shouldn't rely on which observer is notified first.

exercise.prompt: Identify the primary advantage of the Observer pattern.
exercise.starter_code: ans = "It allows for one-to-____ communication where objects stay in sync without knowing each other's internals."
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "many"
assert check("many")
print("PASS")
